I am a results-driven Business Analyst with 4+ years of experience delivering end-to-end SaaS & analytics solutions for globally recognized QSR brands including McDonald's, Taco Bell, Chipotle, Pizza Hut, and Peet's Coffee.

Skilled in requirements elicitation, BRD/FRD/SRS documentation, gap analysis, Agile/Scrum delivery, UAT, and cross-functional stakeholder management. Proficient in SQL, Snowflake, Power BI, Jira, Confluence, and REST API.

Reduced requirement rework by 30%
Zero missed deadlines over 4 years
Reduced post-release defects by 35%
Managed 5+ concurrent product streams

Career Objective

To leverage my expertise in business analysis, data analytics, and agile delivery to drive digital transformation initiatives, optimize business processes, and deliver measurable value to enterprise clients in the QSR and SaaS industries.

RFID-Based Inventory Management

Lead Business Analyst

Problem: Item mismatch issues causing inventory discrepancies across restaurant locations.

Solution: Designed and delivered an RFID-based inventory solution integrating RFID scanners with Inventory and Receiving modules via REST APIs.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Led integration of RFID scanners with backend systems
  • Coordinated end-to-end hardware-to-backend testing
  • Documented API specifications and data flows
REST API Requirements Gathering QA Testing Hardware Middleware

Jaspersoft Reporting Platform

Business Analyst

Problem: Lack of centralized reporting for 500+ restaurant locations with inconsistent KPI tracking.

Solution: Developed KPI-driven Jasper Reports with optimized SQL queries for accuracy and performance across all locations.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Gathered and documented reporting requirements
  • Developed 20+ KPI dashboards and reports
  • Optimized underlying SQL queries for performance
Jasper Reports Advanced SQL Business Intelligence KPI Reporting

AI-Powered Inventory Ordering System

Business Analyst

Problem: Manual, reactive inventory ordering led to frequent stockouts and excess inventory across stores.

Solution: Designed an automated end-to-end ordering pipeline — a scheduled job scans inventory, an AI engine forecasts demand from historical sales, seasonality, and vendor lead time, then drafts a recommended purchase order for manager review before it's sent to the vendor.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Mapped the full process flow from scheduled scan to purchase order generation
  • Defined business rules for par-level checks, safety stock, and validation
  • Specified manager review/approval workflow and PO integration via REST/EDI
Forecasting Python / ML REST API ERP Integration

Data Out — API Migration (SFTP to REST)

Lead Analyst

Problem: Legacy SFTP-based data exchange was slow, error-prone, and difficult to monitor.

Solution: Led migration to real-time REST API architecture with JSON payload contracts and automated monitoring.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Designed JSON payload contracts
  • Built polling and monitoring mechanisms
  • Coordinated endpoint configuration across client environments
API Design JSON Java Data Migration

Store Priming — Copy Store Feature

Business Analyst

Problem: New restaurant locations required manual configuration, causing lengthy onboarding times.

Solution: Conceptualized a 'Copy Store' feature enabling new locations to replicate operational configuration from existing stores.

Key Responsibilities:

Protected Project Details

This project's full architecture & process documentation is shared only with approved viewers. Enter the access password to continue — ask Rishabh directly if you don't have it.

AI Chatbot Process Flow

End-to-end architecture for an enterprise AI chatbot — from user query to final response.

1

User / Channel

The process starts when the user asks a question through any channel such as a web application, mobile app, or WhatsApp.

2

Pre-Pipeline Services

The request first passes through pre-pipeline services that perform basic processing:

  • User authentication
  • Security validation
  • Prompt injection detection
  • Language detection
  • Logging

It also handles simple messages like "Hi," "Hello," or "Thank You" with predefined responses — avoiding unnecessary AI processing and reducing server load.

3

Intent Classification (Categorization)

The chatbot identifies the category or intent of the user's question — HR, IT Support, Finance, or Product Information — so the system can search the correct knowledge base quickly instead of searching everywhere.

4

Query Normalization

The question is cleaned and standardized: spelling mistakes corrected, abbreviations expanded, unnecessary words removed, and the query rewritten into a clear, standard format — improving search accuracy.

5

Cache (CAG) Service

The system checks whether the same or a similar question has already been asked. If yes, the cached answer is returned instantly. If no, the request moves forward. Since frequently asked questions are cached, most common queries are answered instantly — improving response time and reducing AI cost.

6

Hybrid Retrieval Service

If the answer isn't cached, the chatbot combines two search methods:

  • Keyword Search — finds documents containing the exact words
  • Semantic (Vector) Search — finds documents with similar meaning, even with different wording

Results from both are combined for broader, more accurate coverage.

7

Ranker Service

Hybrid retrieval may return multiple documents. The ranker compares them all and selects the most relevant based on relevance score — only the top-ranked documents move forward to the AI model.

8

LLM (Large Language Model)

The LLM receives the user's question along with the top-ranked documents and uses this information to generate a clear, accurate, human-like response.

9

Response to User

Finally, the chatbot sends the generated response back through the same channel the question came from — web, mobile, WhatsApp, or Microsoft Teams.

AI-Powered Inventory Ordering System — Process Flow

Core Competencies

Business Analyst Expertise

Requirement Gathering

Elicitation, analysis, and documentation of business and functional requirements through stakeholder interviews, workshops, and JAD sessions.

BRD & FRD

Comprehensive Business Requirement Documents and Functional Requirement Documents with traceability matrices.

User Stories

Well-crafted user stories with clear acceptance criteria, INVEST principles, and sprint-ready definitions.

Acceptance Criteria

Detailed, testable acceptance criteria ensuring clear definition of done and quality standards.

Process Mapping

As-Is and To-Be process mapping using BPMN, flowcharts, and swimlane diagrams for process optimization.

Gap Analysis

Systematic identification of gaps between current and future states with actionable recommendations.

UAT & Testing

End-to-end UAT framework design, test case creation, execution, and defect management.

Stakeholder Management

Cross-functional stakeholder engagement, C-level communication, and expectation management.

Agile Scrum

Sprint planning, backlog refinement, daily standups, retrospectives, and continuous improvement.

SQL & Data Analysis

Complex SQL queries, data modeling, ETL understanding, and database design concepts.

API Analysis

REST API design, JSON payload validation, Postman testing, and system integration analysis.

Documentation

SOPs, RTMs, release notes, MoMs, and comprehensive technical documentation standards.
